reasoning:
Without the ability to perform a web search to identify the exact artist behind 'The Life of a Showgirl', I must rely on the explicit resolution criteria and base rates for Billboard 200 chart performance. The market rules state: 'If the album does not debut as #1 or is otherwise not released by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket (0).' This condition is extremely strict. It requires two things to avoid '0 weeks': (1) The album must actually be released before the end of 2025, and (2) It MUST debut at #1 (even reaching #1 in its second week would result in 0 weeks according to the text).

Historically, the base rate for any rumored or upcoming album releasing on time is around 60%, as music industry delays are incredibly common. Even if released, debuting at #1 on the Billboard 200 is extremely difficult unless the artist is among the top tier of chart-toppers (like Taylor Swift or Drake). Many major artists debut at #2 or lower due to stiff competition from established albums. Because the album must debut at #1 to avoid resolving to 0, and combined with the persistent risk of postponement past December 2025, the probability that the album either fails to release in time or misses a #1 debut is very high. I estimate this at approximately 82%.
